easyHint: I roll into a ball when threatened, covered in tough scales.
mediumHint: I have no teeth and use a long sticky tongue to catch ants.
hardHint: My scales are made of keratin, the same material as human fingernails, making me heavily trafficked.

easyHint: I am a smiling aquatic creature that never grows up.
mediumHint: I can regenerate my limbs, spinal cord, and even parts of my brain.
hardHint: I am native only to the lake complex of Xochimilco near Mexico City.

easyHint: I look like a zebra on my legs but I'm related to the giraffe.
mediumHint: I secrete a reddish substance from my skin to waterproof my coat.
hardHint: I was unknown to the Western world until 1901 and am found only in the Ituri Rainforest.

easyHint: I am the largest rodent in the world and love to chill in water.
mediumHint: Other animals frequently use me as a piece of living furniture.
hardHint: The Catholic Church once classified me as a fish so I could be eaten during Lent.

easyHint: I am a large flightless bird with a helmet on my head.
mediumHint: My dagger-like claw can fatally wound a predator with one kick.
hardHint: I am known to swallow whole plums and distribute the seeds in my brightly colored droppings.

easyHint: I am Madagascar's top predator, looking like a mix of a cat, dog, and mongoose.
mediumHint: My ankles are fully reversible, allowing me to climb down trees headfirst.
hardHint: I briefly served as the antagonist in a popular 2005 animated movie about escaped zoo animals.

easyHint: I am a crocodile relative with a very long, thin snout.
mediumHint: Males of my species grow a bulbous growth on the tip of their snout called a 'ghara'.
hardHint: I am critically endangered, with my last major stronghold in the Chambal River in India.

easyHint: I am a colorful bird that smells like manure.
mediumHint: My chicks are born with claws on their wings to climb trees.
hardHint: I am uniquely foregut-fermenting, digesting vegetation in my crop much like a cow.

easyHint: I am a heavy, flightless, nocturnal parrot.
mediumHint: When threatened, my main defense mechanism is to freeze completely still.
hardHint: Sirocco, a member of my species, became internet famous after trying to mate with a zoologist's head.

easyHint: I am often called the unicorn of the sea.
mediumHint: My 'horn' is actually a massively enlarged canine tooth.
hardHint: My tusk has millions of nerve endings and might be used to detect changes in water salinity.

easyHint: I am a small Australian marsupial with stripes, and I love eating termites.
mediumHint: Unlike most marsupials, I am diurnal and do not have a proper pouch.
hardHint: I am the faunal emblem of Western Australia and require hollow logs for nesting.

easyHint: I am known as the world's happiest animal due to my permanent smile.
mediumHint: I am a macropod native primarily to Rottnest Island.
hardHint: In extreme danger, mothers of my kind might expel their joey to distract predators.

easyHint: I am an antelope with a very weird, bulbous nose.
mediumHint: My odd nose helps filter out dust in the summer and warm freezing air in the winter.
hardHint: In 2015, over 200,000 of my kind died in a few weeks due to a sudden bacterial outbreak.

easyHint: I am a tiny primate with gigantic eyes.
mediumHint: Each of my eyes is heavier than my brain.
hardHint: I am entirely carnivorous, and some species communicate in pure ultrasound.

easyHint: I look like a lizard but I am actually the last survivor of an ancient reptile order.
mediumHint: I have a 'third eye' on the top of my head when I am young.
hardHint: My lineage split from squamates over 250 million years ago, surviving only in New Zealand.

easyHint: I am the world's smallest and most endangered marine mammal.
mediumHint: I have dark rings around my eyes and a dark patch on my lips.
hardHint: I am often caught in illegal gillnets set for the totoaba fish in the Gulf of California.

easyHint: I am an Australian marsupial that produces cube-shaped poop.
mediumHint: My pouch faces backwards so it doesn't fill with dirt when I dig.
hardHint: My backside is reinforced with cartilage, which I use to crush predators against the roof of my burrow.

easyHint: I am a lemur with a very long, bony middle finger.
mediumHint: I use echolocation to find grubs inside trees and then fish them out with my finger.
hardHint: In Malagasy folklore, I am considered an omen of death, leading to me being killed on sight.

easyHint: I am also known as a bearcat and I smell like hot buttered popcorn.
mediumHint: I am one of the few carnivorans with a fully prehensile tail.
hardHint: The chemical compound 2-AP in my urine is responsible for my distinctive movie-theater aroma.

easyHint: I am a wild cat known for the long, black tufts of hair on my ears.
mediumHint: I can jump up to 10 feet in the air to catch birds mid-flight.
hardHint: In India, I was historically tamed and used for coursing, hunting game birds for royalty.

easyHint: I am a marine mammal similar to a manatee, but with a fluked tail like a dolphin.
mediumHint: I graze strictly on seagrass and am often called a 'sea cow'.
hardHint: My bones are incredibly dense, acting as ballast to help me stay on the sea floor.

easyHint: I am a spiny anteater that lays eggs.
mediumHint: My kind, along with the platypus, are the only surviving monotremes.
hardHint: Males of my species possess a bizarre four-headed reproductive organ.

easyHint: I am a tiny desert fox with incredibly huge ears.
mediumHint: My oversized ears help me dissipate heat and hear prey underground.
hardHint: My paws are heavily furred to act like snowshoes on the scorching Sahara sand.

easyHint: I am a hopping desert rodent that looks like a tiny kangaroo with a long tail.
mediumHint: I do not drink water, getting all my moisture from the food I eat.
hardHint: During WWII, a British armored brigade in North Africa adopted me as their emblem.

easyHint: I am a rainforest mammal with a prehensile tail, often called a 'honey bear'.
mediumHint: I am closely related to raccoons but look and act more like a monkey.
hardHint: Paris Hilton famously kept one of my kind as an exotic pet named Baby Luv.

easyHint: I am a mountainous country in Central Asia, known for yurts and horses.
mediumHint: My flag features a yellow sun with 40 rays representing 40 epic tribes.
hardHint: The Epic of Manas, one of the longest epic poems in the world, is my cultural cornerstone.

easyHint: I am a small country in the Horn of Africa, situated on the Red Sea.
mediumHint: I host military bases for the US, France, Japan, and China.
hardHint: My Lake Assal is the lowest point in Africa and highly saline.

easyHint: I am a small, landlocked African nation formerly known as Swaziland.
mediumHint: I am one of the last absolute monarchies in the world.
hardHint: My king annually selects a new bride during the traditional Umhlanga or Reed Dance.

easyHint: I am a country entirely surrounded by South Africa.
mediumHint: I am the only independent state in the world that lies entirely above 1,000 meters in elevation.
hardHint: I am known for the traditional Basotho blanket and a distinctive conical hat.

easyHint: I am a large Saharan country in West Africa with an Atlantic coastline.
mediumHint: My economy heavily relies on iron ore, transported by one of the longest trains in the world.
hardHint: The ancient Eye of the Sahara (Richat Structure) is prominently visible from space in my territory.

easyHint: I am the smallest island nation in the world.
mediumHint: My economy was once extremely wealthy due to extensive phosphate mining from bird guano.
hardHint: I currently lack a designated capital city, though Yaren acts as the de facto capital.

easyHint: I am a Pacific island nation heavily threatened by rising sea levels.
mediumHint: I generated significant national revenue by leasing out my .tv internet domain.
hardHint: My entire population is around 11,000, and I have no military force whatsoever.

easyHint: I am a South Pacific island nation known for active volcanoes.
mediumHint: The modern extreme sport of bungee jumping originated here as 'land diving'.
hardHint: A unique cargo cult centered around a mythical American WWII serviceman named John Frum exists here.

easyHint: I am a tiny European country nestled between France and Spain.
mediumHint: I am a co-principality, ruled jointly by a Spanish Bishop and the President of France.
hardHint: I am the only country in the world with Catalan as my sole official language.

easyHint: I am a Central American country known for my Great Blue Hole.
mediumHint: I am the only Central American country where English is the official language.
hardHint: My former capital was devastated by a hurricane in 1961, forcing a move to Belmopan.

easyHint: I am a Himalayan kingdom known for measuring Gross National Happiness.
mediumHint: I am the only carbon-negative country in the world.
hardHint: It is completely illegal to buy or sell tobacco in my territory, though consumption has complex rules.

easyHint: I am a volcanic archipelago nation off the coast of East Africa.
mediumHint: I am often referred to as the 'Perfume Islands' due to ylang-ylang cultivation.
hardHint: I experienced more than 20 coups or attempted coups between independence in 1975 and 2008.

easyHint: I am a country in the Horn of Africa, bordering the Red Sea and Ethiopia.
mediumHint: My capital, Asmara, is known for its well-preserved Italian modernist architecture.
hardHint: I am often called the 'North Korea of Africa' due to my highly secretive and authoritarian regime.

easyHint: I am a Central African country located on the equator, rich in oil.
mediumHint: Over 80% of my land is covered in rainforest, home to many western lowland gorillas.
hardHint: A natural nuclear fission reactor operated in my Oklo region roughly 2 billion years ago.

easyHint: I am a small West African country with a Portuguese colonial past.
mediumHint: My Bijagos archipelago is a matriarchal society and a UNESCO biosphere reserve.
hardHint: In the early 2000s, I tragically became known as Africa's first 'narco-state'.

easyHint: I am a sprawling Pacific nation composed mostly of atolls.
mediumHint: I am the only country situated in all four hemispheres of the globe.
hardHint: To unify our time zones, we skipped December 31, 1994, entirely.

easyHint: I am a tiny alpine country sandwiched between Switzerland and Austria.
mediumHint: I am a 'doubly landlocked' country.
hardHint: During the Austro-Prussian war, our 80-man army famously returned with 81 men because they made a friend.

easyHint: I am an Eastern European country wedged between Romania and Ukraine.
mediumHint: I host the world's largest wine cellar, Milestii Mici.
hardHint: A breakaway region within my borders, Transnistria, uses a hammer and sickle on its flag.

easyHint: I am a Pacific island nation known for incredible scuba diving.
mediumHint: I have a famous marine lake entirely filled with stingless jellyfish.
hardHint: I require visitors to sign an 'eco-pledge' stamped directly into their passports.

easyHint: I am a microstate completely surrounded by Italy.
mediumHint: I claim to be the world's oldest surviving sovereign state and constitutional republic.
hardHint: My head of state consists of two 'Captains Regent' who serve concurrently for only six months.

easyHint: I am a two-island nation off the western equatorial coast of Africa.
mediumHint: I am the second-smallest African country by population, known for excellent chocolate.
hardHint: The equator line famously passes directly through my Ilheu das Rolas islet.

easyHint: I am a stunning archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean, east of mainland Africa.
mediumHint: I am home to the giant Aldabra tortoise.
hardHint: The rare Coco de Mer palm, which produces the heaviest seed in the world, grows naturally only here.

easyHint: I am a small country on the northeastern coast of South America.
mediumHint: I am the only sovereign state outside Europe where Dutch is the official and prevailing language.
hardHint: My population includes a massive demographic of Javanese and Hindustani people due to colonial indentured labor.

easyHint: I am a mountainous country in Central Asia bordering Afghanistan and China.
mediumHint: My capital city's name literally translates to 'Monday' in Persian.
hardHint: The Pamir Highway, a major logistical feat of the Soviet era, traverses my rugged terrain.

easyHint: I am a narrow, elongated country in West Africa on the Gulf of Guinea.
mediumHint: My capital is home to one of the world's largest Voodoo fetishes markets.
hardHint: I was uniquely split between the British and French after World War I.

easyHint: I am an experimental township in South India dedicated to human unity.
mediumHint: At my center stands the Matrimandir, a massive golden sphere.
hardHint: I was designed by architect Roger Anger and officially inaugurated by 'The Mother' in 1968.

easyHint: I am an ancient village in Karnataka filled with magnificent temple ruins.
mediumHint: I was once the capital of the formidable Vijayanagara Empire.
hardHint: The musical pillars of my Vittala Temple produce distinct chimes when struck.

easyHint: I am a town in Madhya Pradesh famous for temples with erotic sculptures.
mediumHint: My temples were built by the Chandela dynasty between 950 and 1050 AD.
hardHint: Despite the fame of the sensuous carvings, they make up less than 10% of the total artwork here.

easyHint: I am a picturesque valley town in Arunachal Pradesh.
mediumHint: I am famous for an annual independent music festival that bears my name.
hardHint: I am the home of the Apatani tribe, known historically for their facial tattoos and nose plugs.

easyHint: I am the largest river island in the world, located in Assam.
mediumHint: I sit right in the middle of the mighty Brahmaputra river.
hardHint: I am a hub of Neo-Vaishnavite culture, sustained by institutions called Satras.

easyHint: I am an ancient archaeological site in the Rann of Kutch.
mediumHint: I am one of the five largest Harappan (Indus Valley) sites.
hardHint: A unique signboard with ten large Indus script characters was discovered at my northern gate.

easyHint: I am an abandoned town at the south-eastern tip of Pamban Island.
mediumHint: I was entirely destroyed by the 1964 Rameswaram cyclone.
hardHint: According to legend, this is exactly where Lord Rama pointed his bow to build the bridge to Lanka.

easyHint: I am a region in Tamil Nadu renowned for my fiery cuisine and palatial homes.
mediumHint: My heritage mansions are built using materials imported from all over the world during the colonial era.
hardHint: My local plastering technique uses a unique mixture containing egg whites to keep the walls cool.

easyHint: I am a cold desert mountain valley located high in the Himalayas.
mediumHint: My name translates to 'The Middle Land', as I lie between Tibet and India.
hardHint: I house the Key Monastery and the highest post office in the world at Hikkim.

easyHint: I am a temple town in Karnataka that has become a popular beach destination.
mediumHint: My famous Mahabaleshwar temple houses the 'Atmalinga'.
hardHint: My beaches are uniquely shaped, with one famously resembling the symbol Om.

easyHint: I am a village in Meghalaya, known for holding a very wet world record.
mediumHint: I receive the highest average annual rainfall on Earth.
hardHint: My inhabitants historically wear 'knups', umbrella-like shields made of bamboo and reeds, to survive the downpours.

easyHint: I am a city in Rajasthan known for my massive, imposing fort.
mediumHint: I was the capital of Mewar and the site of three historical sieges.
hardHint: My fort contains the striking 'Vijay Stambha' (Tower of Victory) erected by Rana Kumbha.

easyHint: I am a coastal city in Karnataka famous for my vegetarian cuisine.
mediumHint: I am home to the Krishna Matha, where devotees view the deity through a silver-plated window.
hardHint: The modern Masala Dosa's widespread commercialization is heavily credited to my region's hoteliers.

easyHint: I am known as the 'Golden City' of India.
mediumHint: My massive fort is one of the few 'living forts' in the world, with a quarter of my population still residing inside.
hardHint: I was established by Rawal Jaisal and built entirely out of yellow sandstone.

easyHint: I am the city in Haryana where the great Mahabharata war was fought.
mediumHint: Lord Krishna delivered the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna on my soil.
hardHint: My Brahma Sarovar water tank is supposedly the site where Lord Brahma created the universe.

easyHint: I am an ancient city in Bihar famous for my historical university.
mediumHint: My ancient university attracted scholars from Tibet, China, and Korea.
hardHint: My library, Dharmaganja, was so vast that it reportedly burned for months when sacked by Bakhtiyar Khalji.

easyHint: I am the most important Buddhist pilgrimage site in the world.
mediumHint: I am the place where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment.
hardHint: A descendant of the original Peepal tree under which the Buddha sat still grows here today.

easyHint: I am a city near Varanasi where Buddha gave his first sermon.
mediumHint: The national emblem of India, the Lion Capital, was discovered here.
hardHint: Emperor Ashoka erected a massive stupa here called the Dhamek Stupa.

easyHint: I am a town in Madhya Pradesh known for my grand palaces on the Betwa River.
mediumHint: I was founded in the 16th century by the Bundela Rajput chief, Rudra Pratap.
hardHint: The Jahangir Mahal here was built specifically to host the Mughal Emperor for a single night.

easyHint: I am a town in Karnataka that was the regal capital of the Hoysala Empire.
mediumHint: My name literally translates to 'ruined city' in Kannada.
hardHint: My Hoysaleswara temple features intricate soapstone carvings and was looted twice by the Delhi Sultanate.

easyHint: I am an important Jain pilgrimage center in Karnataka.
mediumHint: I am famous for the massive monolithic statue of Gommateshwara (Bahubali).
hardHint: Every 12 years, the Mahamastakabhisheka festival is held here, anointing the statue with milk, saffron, and ghee.

easyHint: I am a ruined fortress city in Madhya Pradesh, famed for my Afghan architecture.
mediumHint: I am associated with the legendary romance of Baz Bahadur and Rani Roopmati.
hardHint: Hoshang Shah's tomb here is said to be India's first marble edifice, inspiring the Taj Mahal.

easyHint: I am a historical town in Madhya Pradesh, highly famous for my handloom sarees.
mediumHint: I am surrounded by hills, lakes, and forests, heavily mentioned in the Mahabharata.
hardHint: The infamous battle of 1528 saw the Rajput women here commit mass Jauhar when Babur captured the fort.

easyHint: I am a site in Madhya Pradesh famous for my ancient rock shelters.
mediumHint: I house some of the oldest prehistoric cave paintings in India.
hardHint: My name is derived from a legend linking me to the 'sitting place of Bhima' from the Mahabharata.

easyHint: I am one of the most prominent ancient cities of the Indus Valley Civilization, located in Gujarat.
mediumHint: I am best known for having the world's earliest known dockyard.
hardHint: My bead-making industry was so advanced that my products reached as far as Sumer in Mesopotamia.

easyHint: I was a brilliant Indian mathematician who largely taught himself.
mediumHint: I collaborated closely with G. H. Hardy at Cambridge University.
hardHint: I famously noted that 1729 is the smallest number expressible as the sum of two cubes in two different ways.

easyHint: I am widely considered the father of the Indian nuclear program.
mediumHint: I founded both TIFR and the atomic energy establishment in Trombay.
hardHint: I tragically died in a mysterious plane crash on Mont Blanc in 1966.

easyHint: I am an Indian physicist who won the Nobel Prize for studying the scattering of light.
mediumHint: My discovery explains why the sky and the ocean are blue.
hardHint: I was the first Asian to receive a Nobel Prize in any branch of science.

easyHint: I am an Indian physicist whose name is given to a class of subatomic particles.
mediumHint: I collaborated with Albert Einstein on a specific state of matter.
hardHint: Despite my foundational work on quantum statistics, I was never awarded a Nobel Prize.

easyHint: I am widely regarded as the father of the Indian space program.
mediumHint: I helped establish ISRO and the physical research laboratory in Ahmedabad.
hardHint: I was married to the famous classical dancer Mrinalini Sarabhai.

easyHint: I was known as the 'Missile Man of India' and became President.
mediumHint: I played a pivotal role in the Pokhran-II nuclear tests.
hardHint: I collapsed and died while delivering a lecture at IIM Shillong.

easyHint: I was the first woman of Indian origin to go to space.
mediumHint: I flew on Space Shuttle Columbia in 1997 and 2003.
hardHint: A meteorological satellite, METSAT-1, was renamed in my honor shortly after my death.

easyHint: I am an Indian economist and philosopher who won a Nobel Memorial Prize.
mediumHint: My work focuses heavily on welfare economics and the mechanics of famine.
hardHint: I famously argued that no substantial famine has ever occurred in an independent, democratic country.

easyHint: I am a Bengali poet who composed the national anthem of India.
mediumHint: I also composed the national anthem for Bangladesh.
hardHint: I repudiated my British knighthood in protest against the Jallianwala Bagh massacre.

easyHint: I am one of the most celebrated writers of Hindustani literature.
mediumHint: My real name was Dhanpat Rai Srivastava.
hardHint: My iconic novel 'Godaan' heavily critiques the socio-economic exploitation of the Indian peasantry.

easyHint: I am a famous Indian author who created the fictional town of Malgudi.
mediumHint: My most famous novel, 'The Guide', was adapted into a classic Bollywood film.
hardHint: My close friend, the British author Graham Greene, helped me get my first book published.

easyHint: I am a British-Indian author known for my beloved children's books set in the Himalayas.
mediumHint: I wrote 'The Room on the Roof' when I was just 17.
hardHint: I have lived in Landour, near Mussoorie, for decades, becoming a fixture of the local culture.

easyHint: I am an Indian painter known for depicting Hindu deities in a European academic art style.
mediumHint: My lithographic presses made my artwork available to the Indian masses.
hardHint: My depiction of Shakuntala is one of the most iconic images in modern Indian art history.

easyHint: I was a pioneering Indian-Hungarian painter often called the 'Frida Kahlo of India'.
mediumHint: My work documented the lives of impoverished Indian villagers in the 1930s.
hardHint: My painting 'Village Scene' remains one of the most expensive works of Indian art ever sold.

easyHint: I was a highly controversial and celebrated modern Indian painter.
mediumHint: I was famous for depicting horses and for walking barefoot everywhere.
hardHint: I spent my final years in self-imposed exile in Qatar due to intense backlash over my nude depictions of deities.

easyHint: I am widely considered the 'Nightingale of India'.
mediumHint: I recorded playback singing for thousands of Bollywood films over seven decades.
hardHint: The song 'Aye Mere Watan Ke Logo', sung by me, famously moved Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru to tears.

easyHint: I was a legendary playback singer and actor known for my eccentric, joyful voice.
mediumHint: I often yodeled in my popular Bollywood songs.
hardHint: I banned the state broadcaster AIR and Doordarshan from playing my songs during the Emergency after refusing to sing for a political rally.

easyHint: I am a classic playback singer known for my incredible vocal range and versatility.
mediumHint: I was the dominant voice of Hindi cinema in the 1960s before Kishore Kumar's rise.
hardHint: I famously sang 'Man Tarpat Hari Darshan Ko Aaj', a deeply Hindu devotional song written and composed entirely by Muslims.

easyHint: I was a legendary Carnatic vocalist from Madurai.
mediumHint: I was the first musician ever to be awarded the Bharat Ratna.
hardHint: I famously performed at the UN General Assembly in 1966.

easyHint: I was an Indian sitar virtuoso who popularized Indian classical music globally.
mediumHint: I heavily influenced George Harrison of The Beatles.
hardHint: I famously performed a legendary, rain-soaked set at the 1969 Woodstock festival.

easyHint: I was a legendary musician who elevated the shehnai to a concert instrument.
mediumHint: I played at the Red Fort on the eve of India's independence in 1947.
hardHint: I deeply refused to settle in the US because I could not imagine living without the river Ganga.

easyHint: I am the most famous contemporary tabla virtuoso in the world.
mediumHint: My curly hair and charismatic smile are iconic, and I starred in a famous Taj Mahal tea commercial.
hardHint: I was a founding member of the influential world music group 'Shakti' alongside John McLaughlin.

easyHint: I am a legendary Bengali filmmaker who won an honorary Oscar.
mediumHint: My debut film was the iconic 'Pather Panchali'.
hardHint: I designed my own distinct typefaces, including one named 'Ray Roman'.

easyHint: I was a classic Bollywood director and actor known for my tragic, soulful films.
mediumHint: My masterpieces include 'Pyaasa' and 'Kaagaz Ke Phool'.
hardHint: The commercial failure of my semi-autobiographical film deeply impacted my mental health, leading to my tragic early death.

easyHint: I was a pioneering Hindi film director noted for my realistic, socially conscious cinema.
mediumHint: I directed the iconic films 'Do Bigha Zamin' and 'Madhumati'.
hardHint: I brought the neo-realist movement to Indian cinema, heavily influenced by Vittorio De Sica's 'Bicycle Thieves'.

easyHint: I am a small metal loop used to temporarily hold clothes together.
mediumHint: I have a clasp that covers my sharp point to protect the user.
hardHint: I was invented in 1849 by Walter Hunt to settle a 15-dollar debt.

easyHint: I am a small container filled with tiny sticks that make fire.
mediumHint: My sides have a striking surface made of red phosphorus.
hardHint: I often feature vintage, highly collectible graphic art labels originating from Sweden and India.

easyHint: I am a bent piece of wire used to hold sheets together.
mediumHint: I am the classic shape known as the 'Gem' design.
hardHint: During WWII, Norwegians wore me on their lapels as a subtle symbol of resistance against Nazi occupation.

easyHint: I use interlocking teeth to close jackets and bags.
mediumHint: I was originally marketed under the clunky name 'clasp locker'.
hardHint: The ubiquitous YKK stamped on my slider stands for Yoshida Kogyo Kabushikigaisha.

easyHint: I am a fabric hook-and-loop fastener that makes a tearing sound.
mediumHint: I am often used on children's shoes instead of laces.
hardHint: I was invented by a Swiss engineer who noticed burrs sticking to his dog's fur after a walk.

easyHint: I am a stretchy loop used to hold things together.
mediumHint: I am often used to snap people or create a makeshift slingshot.
hardHint: I degrade and become a sticky, brittle mess if left out in the sun or heat for too long.

easyHint: I punch metal wire through paper and fold it to bind pages.
mediumHint: My most famous red version was fiercely protected in the movie 'Office Space'.
hardHint: My anvil plate can usually be rotated to pin documents temporarily outward instead of binding them inward.

easyHint: I am a small tool that shaves wood to reveal graphite.
mediumHint: I use a small, single razor blade set at an angle.
hardHint: Before my widespread invention, people strictly used a small knife (hence the term 'penknife') for my job.

easyHint: I am a small rubbery block used to remove pencil marks.
mediumHint: I often sit at the end of a pencil, bound by a metal ferrule.
hardHint: Before my invention, people commonly used crustless bread to perform my primary function.

easyHint: I am a small metal cup worn on the finger while sewing.
mediumHint: I have dimples on my surface to prevent a needle from slipping.
hardHint: I am historically used as a token in the board game Monopoly.

easyHint: I am a string used to secure footwear.
mediumHint: I am tied in a bow and often come undone if not double-knotted.
hardHint: The small plastic or metal sheath at my very end is called an aglet.

easyHint: I am a small disc sewn onto shirts that slips through a slit to close it.
mediumHint: I traditionally have either two or four small holes in the center.
hardHint: Historically, men's clothing has me on the right, while women's clothing places me on the left.

easyHint: I am a flat tool with teeth used to untangle hair.
mediumHint: I am often carried in a back pocket and run through grease.
hardHint: I am one of the oldest tools found by archaeologists, historically made from tortoise shell or bone.

easyHint: I am a small brush used with paste to clean your mouth.
mediumHint: I should be replaced every three to four months as my bristles fray.
hardHint: The first mass-produced version of me used stiff hog bristles attached to a bone handle.

easyHint: I am a small mechanical tool used to trim your fingertips.
mediumHint: I use a compound lever design to apply cutting force.
hardHint: I often include a small, fold-out file with a hooked end for cleaning dirt.

easyHint: I am a small pincer used to pluck hairs or remove splinters.
mediumHint: I rely on spring tension and require squeezing to close.
hardHint: Philatelists (stamp collectors) use a specialized, smooth-spaded version of me to avoid damaging paper.

easyHint: I am a helical metal tool used to open wine bottles.
mediumHint: I often feature levers that look like arms rising as the screw goes down.
hardHint: The central helical metal piece that does the actual work is properly known as the 'worm'.

easyHint: I am a tool used to remove metal caps from glass drinks.
mediumHint: I am frequently attached to keychains as a handy novelty item.
hardHint: My standard design functions as a second-class lever resting on the center of the crown cap.

easyHint: I am a kitchen tool used to puncture and remove metal lids.
mediumHint: I usually feature two gears and a cutting wheel that you manually crank.
hardHint: I was not invented until nearly 50 years after my target object; before me, people used a hammer and chisel.

easyHint: I am a kitchen utensil with a broad, flat blade, used for flipping pancakes.
mediumHint: My blade is often slotted to let grease drain away.
hardHint: Spongebob Squarepants famously wields me at his job at the Krusty Krab.

easyHint: I am a cooking tool made of looped wires, used for beating eggs.
mediumHint: I am essential for incorporating air into heavy cream or egg whites.
hardHint: The classic balloon shape is the most common, but French and flat versions exist for specific sauces.

easyHint: I am a kitchen tool used to remove the skin from potatoes or carrots.
mediumHint: I usually have a swiveling blade to follow the contours of the vegetable.
hardHint: I often feature a small, sharpened loop at the tip meant for gouging out potato 'eyes'.

easyHint: I am a metal kitchen tool with sharp holes used to shred cheese.
mediumHint: I often come in a four-sided box shape with different sized holes.
hardHint: If you are not careful, you might accidentally scrape your knuckles on my surface while prepping dinner.

easyHint: I am a wire mesh bowl used to drain pasta or wash vegetables.
mediumHint: I am sometimes called a colander, especially when shaped like a large bowl.
hardHint: Followers of the satirical religion 'Pastafarianism' famously wear me on their heads.

easyHint: I am a wide-mouthed tube that narrows at the bottom, used to pour liquids.
mediumHint: I am crucial when trying to pour oil into a car's engine without spilling.
hardHint: The Tin Woodman from The Wizard of Oz wears an inverted version of me as a hat.
